design_philosophy.core_loop: Select one of four Avengers characters, progress through side-scrolling levels fighting generic enemies and supervillain bosses, interspersed with flying/shooting sequences. Each character has melee attacks, throwable items, and character-specific special 'Avenger Attacks' (projectiles or energy beams).
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing character-specific combat abilities against varied enemy types and boss patterns while surviving arcade-style difficulty through attrition and resource management.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as iconic Marvel Avengers (Captain America, Iron Man, Hawkeye, Vision) fighting through Red Skull's army to save the world, with cooperative multiplayer enabling team-based superhero action.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Port of arcade beat-em-up maintains arcade design philosophy: character asymmetry (melee vs ranged, terrestrial vs flying), mandatory special attack resource management, and progression through gauntlet of themed bosses. No difficulty options or balancing toward home console audiences.

difficulty.lives_system: Arcade-style lives system carried from original arcade; limited continues
difficulty.continues_system: Continues system present, typical of arcade ports
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based progression with stage gates; no mid-level checkpoints
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Minimal; character selection provides asymmetric gameplay (ranged vs melee, speed vs power) but no difficulty slider or adaptive systems

progression.unlock_mechanism: Linear stage progression; character selection at start determines moveset availability throughout
replayability_hooks: Four distinct playable characters (Captain America, Iron Man, Hawkeye, Vision) with different attack patterns and special abilities
Asymmetric aerial mechanics (Iron Man/Vision fly natively; Captain America/Hawkeye use vehicles)
Cooperative multiplayer encourages replaying with different character combinations
Boss pattern variation based on character matchups

world_building_techniques: Character roster selection screen establishes Marvel universe presence
Boss encounters feature supervillain gallery from Marvel canon (Klaw, Living Laser, Whirlwind, Sentinel, Wizard, Grim Reaper, Mandarin, Juggernaut, Ultron, Controller, Crossbones, Red Skull)
Supporting character cameos (Wasp, Quicksilver, Wonder Man, Namor) assist during levels
Red Skull as final antagonist provides overarching threat narrative

boss_design.philosophy: Arcade gauntlet design: parade of themed supervillain encounters with distinct visual silhouettes and combat patterns. Boss introductions through stage progression create variety and skill escalation.

faction_indicators: Red Skull's villain coalition (assembled army of supervillains)
Avengers team identity (four-hero roster with distinct visual designs)
Supporting Marvel characters as allied forces
danger_signals: Generic henchmen as foot soldiers indicating organized threat
Progressive supervillain escalation from minor (Klaw, Living Laser) to cosmic threats (Ultron, Mandarin)
Red Skull as world conquest antagonist
environmental_storytelling: Stage theming based on villain associations and Marvel locations
Flying sequences indicate escalating threat scope
Boss chamber progression visualizes Red Skull's resource investment

sources_used: tcrf - Captain America and The Avengers (SNES) development credits and technical data
wikipedia - Comprehensive gameplay mechanics, character roster, boss list, version differences, and arcade/home release information
